frozen_components: SLM encoder pretrained on high-resource domain
architecture.summary: LS-CLDARE is a collaborative cross-lingual data augmentation framework for entity relationship extraction in low-resource languages. An SLM extracts entity information from source text; an LLM uses chain-of-thought prompts to generate cross-lingual augmented samples by combining entity information with high-resource language descriptions. The generated samples are combined with cross-lingual soft prompts and fed into an SLM pretrained on high-resource domain data. Transfer learning and iterative data expansion improve entity recognition and relation extraction for low-resource target languages.
trained_components: soft prompt parameters for cross-lingual transfer
SLM fine-tuning on augmented cross-lingual data

biblio.title: Large and Small models for collaborative cross-lingual data augmentation in entity relationship extraction for low-resource languages
biblio.venue: Journal of King Saud University - Computer and Information Sciences

claims.statement: F1 score improved by 3.52% to 9.42% compared with other data augmentation methods
F1 score improved by 1.8% to 16.93% compared with other prompt-based relation extraction methods
LLM-based data augmentation reduces reliance on manual evaluations and generates more fluent and diverse data compared with traditional DA methods
Cross-lingual augmentation methods more effectively enhance the diversity of data in low-resource language settings by leveraging and extending data resources derived from other languages
Chain-of-thought prompts guide the LLM through step-by-step reasoning to better handle complex entity relationship extraction tasks
